column5: A/B Prototype Test Creation involves designing and comparing different versions of prototypes, such as low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes and wireframes, to evaluate which design best meets user needs and improves user experience. This duty requires skills like attention to detail, critical thinking, problem-solving, and effective communication to create clear, testable concepts and gather meaningful feedback. Practices such as decision-making, ownership, psychological safety, and empowerment ensure a supportive environment where ideas can be tested and refined confidently. Strong organization, perseverance, and collaboration help manage time and resources efficiently, while contextual inquiries and concept testing provide valuable insights into user behavior. These combined skills and practices directly impact the success of the activity by enabling accurate evaluation of design options, leading to better user-centered solutions in UX research.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ab-testing/ (Nielsen Norman Group: A/B Testing)  
2. https://www.optimizely.com/optimization-glossary/ab-testing/ (Optimizely: A/B Testing Guide)  
3. https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2018/01/ab-testing-guide/ (Smashing Magazine: A Comprehensive Guide To A/B Testing)
column7: A/B Prototype Test Creation is valuable for teams because it helps them make things like Low-Fidelity Prototypes, High-Fidelity Prototypes, and Wireframes, which are important for designing and improving how people use a product or service during the Experience Design milestone. This activity lets teams try out different ideas and see which one works best, making sure the final product is easy and fun to use. The duty of UX (User Experience) Research is to guide this process, so the team can reach important points, or milestones, in their project, like creating and testing new designs.
column8: The UX Research duty collaborates closely with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties to deliver low-fidelity prototypes, high-fidelity prototypes, and wireframes during the Experience Design milestones. UX Research gathers and interprets user insights, which inform the Business Analysis duty in defining requirements and the Product Management and Product Ownership duties in prioritizing features and aligning objectives. UX Design and UX Strategy duties use these insights and requirements to create and refine prototype variations. Through clear communication and shared practices like ownership and empowerment, these duties ensure that each prototype is tested, evaluated, and iterated upon, resulting in deliverables that effectively address user needs and support informed design decisions.
column9:
To be great at A/B Prototype Test Creation, the UX Research duty needs strong skills in attention to detail, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making. These skills help in designing clear and effective test variations, accurately interpreting user feedback, and making informed choices about which prototype best meets user needs. Practices like ownership, empowerment, psychological safety, and self-organization are essential for working smoothly with other cross-functional duties such as Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy. Clear verbal and written communication, collaboration, and time management ensure that insights are shared and understood by all involved duties.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering low-fidelity prototypes, high-fidelity prototypes, and wireframes within the Experience Design milestones. When the UX Research duty applies these abilities, it leads to thorough concept testing and contextual inquiries, which provide valuable insights for other duties to define requirements, prioritize features, and refine designs. This organized and empowered way of working helps ensure that deliverables are completed on time, meet user needs, and support well-informed design decisions, ultimately improving the overall user experience.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Research duty carries out the activity A/B Prototype Test Creation with others  
Step 1  
UX Research gathers user insights through contextual inquiries and concept testing, then shares these findings with Business Analysis, Product Management, and Product Ownership duties to help define requirements and prioritize features for the prototypes.  

Step 2  
UX Design and UX Strategy duties use the requirements and user insights to create low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes and wireframes. UX Research collaborates by providing feedback and ensuring the prototypes address user needs.  

Step 3  
UX Research organizes and conducts A/B prototype tests, analyzes the results, and communicates findings to all involved duties. This enables informed decision-making and further refinement of prototypes, ensuring the final deliverables effectively improve user experience.
column11:
The UX (User Experience) Research duty is both responsible for and accountable to the success of A/B Prototype Test Creation, meaning this duty designs, conducts, and oversees the testing of different prototype versions to determine which best meets user needs. Product Ownership, Product Management, UX Strategy, UX Design, Business Analysis, and UX Writing duties are consulted throughout the process, providing advice and feedback to ensure the prototypes align with business goals, user requirements, and effective communication. After the activity is completed, a wide range of duties—including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching—are informed about the outcomes and findings.

All these duties work together by sharing insights, requirements, and feedback at each stage of prototype creation and testing. The responsible and accountable UX Research duty gathers and interprets user data, while consulted duties help refine the prototypes and ensure alignment with objectives. Once the testing is complete, informing the broader set of duties ensures everyone is aware of the results and can use this knowledge to guide future decisions. This collaborative approach, supported by clear communication and shared practices like ownership and empowerment, leads to well-informed design choices that improve user experience.

column5: Analyzing Project Risk involves carefully identifying and evaluating potential problems that could affect the success of a project. This duty requires skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, decision-making, and organization to thoroughly assess risks and develop strategies to manage them. Practices like ownership, empowerment, and psychological safety encourage taking responsibility and open communication, which help in making informed decisions and fostering a supportive environment for risk analysis. Deliverables include a detailed Project Plan and Risk Analysis that guide the project through potential challenges. The combination of these skills and practices ensures risks are anticipated and managed effectively, increasing the likelihood of project success.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. Project Management Institute (PMI) – "Risk Management" (https://www.pmi.org/learning/library/risk-management-projects-8323)  
2. MindTools – "Risk Analysis and Risk Management" (https://www.mindtools.com/a5v9v4g/risk-analysis)  
3. Association for Project Management (APM) – "What is Risk Management?" (https://www.apm.org.uk/resources/find-a-resource/risk-management/)
column7: Analyzing Project Risk is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like a Project Plan and Risk Analysis, which are important for making sure everyone knows what could go wrong and how to handle it during milestones such as Intake and all Product Milestones. This activity helps teams spot problems early and make plans to avoid or fix them, so the project can stay on track and reach its goals.
column8: The duty owner for Project Management works closely with Product Management and Product Ownership duties to deliver the Project Plan and Risk Analysis needed for each milestone, from Intake through all Product Milestones. Project Management gathers information from Product Management about goals, requirements, and market factors, and collaborates with Product Ownership to understand technical constraints and priorities. By sharing insights and openly discussing potential risks, these duties ensure that all possible challenges are identified and documented. This cooperation allows for clear planning and proactive risk management, helping the project stay on track and increasing the chances of success at every milestone.
column9:
To be great at analyzing project risk while working with other cross-functional duties, the Project Management duty needs strong attention to detail, critical thinking, and decision-making skills. Organization and perseverance are essential for documenting risks clearly and following through on risk management plans. Effective verbal and written communication, collaboration, and logical thinking help gather information from Product Management and Product Ownership, ensuring all potential risks are identified and understood. Practices like ownership, empowerment, and psychological safety encourage open discussion of risks and support a culture where everyone feels comfortable sharing concerns and solutions.

These skills and practices directly impact the success of delivering the Project Plan and Risk Analysis within given milestones. By proactively identifying and addressing risks, the Project Management duty helps prevent problems that could delay progress or affect quality. Clear communication and collaboration with other duties ensure that all requirements and constraints are considered, making the planning process more accurate and reliable. This approach increases the likelihood of meeting each milestone on time and achieving overall project success.
column10:
Here's how the Project Management duty carries out the activity Analyzing Project Risk with others  
Step 1  
Project Management gathers information from Product Management about project goals, requirements, and market factors, and collaborates with Product Ownership to understand technical constraints and priorities.  

Step 2  
Project Management uses critical thinking and attention to detail to identify and evaluate potential risks, openly discussing these with Product Management and Product Ownership to ensure all possible challenges are considered and documented in the Project Plan and Risk Analysis.  

Step 3  
Project Management communicates the identified risks and proposed solutions, encourages ownership and psychological safety so all duties feel empowered to share concerns, and updates the risk analysis as the project progresses through each milestone, ensuring proactive risk management and continuous improvement.
column11:
For Analyzing Project Risk, the Project Management duty is both responsible for performing the analysis and accountable for ensuring its success. Product Management oversees the process, while Product Ownership, Scrum Master, Business Analysis, UX Design, UX Research, UX Writing, UX Strategy, Agile Coaching, Account Management, and Product Management duties are consulted to provide advice and feedback. All duties, including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching, are informed about the work after it is completed.

These duties work together by having Project Management lead the identification and evaluation of risks, gathering insights from consulted duties to ensure all potential challenges are considered. Consulted duties share their expertise and feedback, helping to create a thorough and realistic risk analysis. Once the analysis is complete, all relevant duties are informed, ensuring everyone understands the risks and planned solutions. This clear division of responsibility, accountability, consultation, and information sharing helps the project stay organized, proactive, and more likely to achieve its milestones.

column5: Analyzing Research Findings involves carefully examining data collected from user studies to identify patterns, insights, and usability issues using skills like affinity mapping, data analysis, critical thinking, and attention to detail. This duty requires practices such as decision-making, ownership, and empowerment to ensure thorough and accurate interpretation of results, which are then organized into deliverables like research reports, rainbow tables, and usability test reports. Effective collaboration, communication, and time management enhance the clarity and impact of the findings, directly influencing the success of the UX research by providing actionable insights that guide design improvements and user experience enhancements.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. https://www.coursera.org/learn/research-methods (Coursera: Understanding Research Methods)  
2. https://guides.lib.unc.edu/critically-analyzing-information/research-articles (UNC Libraries: Critically Analyzing Information Sources)  
3. https://writingcenter.unc.edu/tips-and-tools/literature-reviews/ (UNC Writing Center: Literature Reviews)
column7: Analyzing Research Findings is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps achieve deliverables like a Research Report, Research Analysis, Rainbow Table, and Usability Test Report, which help a team understand what users need and how they use a product during milestones such as Continuous Discovery and Acceptance Testing. This activity lets the duty look at all the information collected from users, find important patterns, and share what they learn so everyone can make better decisions and build things that people actually want and can use easily.
column8: The UX Research duty works closely with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties to deliver key research deliverables throughout the project milestones. While UX Research focuses on gathering and analyzing user data, Business Analysis helps interpret findings in the context of business goals, and Product Management and Product Ownership ensure that insights align with product direction and priorities. UX Design and UX Strategy duties use the research analysis, research report, rainbow table, and usability test report to inform design decisions and strategic planning. By collaborating and sharing findings, each duty contributes its expertise to ensure that the deliverables are accurate, actionable, and support continuous discovery and acceptance testing, ultimately leading to a better user experience.
column9:
To be great at analyzing research findings while working with other cross-functional duties, the UX Research duty needs strong skills in affinity mapping, data analysis, critical thinking, and attention to detail. Practices such as decision-making, ownership, and continuous growth are essential for thoroughly interpreting user data and identifying meaningful patterns. Effective collaboration, organization, and perseverance help ensure that findings are clearly communicated through written and verbal communication, while psychological safety and empowerment encourage open discussion and innovative thinking with other duties like Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering research reports, research analysis, rainbow tables, and usability test reports within given milestones. By using critical thinking and clear communication, the UX Research duty provides actionable insights that align with business goals and product direction, supporting continuous discovery and acceptance testing. Strong organization and time management help meet deadlines, while collaboration and psychological safety ensure that findings are shared and used effectively by other duties, leading to better user experience design and successful project outcomes.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Research duty carries out the activity Analyzing Research Findings with others  
Step 1  
The UX Research duty gathers and organizes all collected user data, using skills like affinity mapping and data analysis to identify patterns and key insights. During this step, the duty collaborates with Business Analysis to ensure findings are interpreted in the context of business goals and with Product Management and Product Ownership to confirm that insights align with product direction and priorities.

Step 2  
The UX Research duty synthesizes the data into clear research reports and analysis, using critical thinking and attention to detail. These deliverables are then shared with UX Design and UX Strategy duties, who use the findings to inform design decisions and strategic planning. Open discussion and psychological safety are encouraged to empower all duties to ask questions and suggest ideas, ensuring the insights are actionable and support continuous discovery and acceptance testing.

Step 3  
The UX Research duty facilitates communication and feedback sessions, using verbal and written communication skills to present findings and answer questions from other cross-functional duties. This ongoing collaboration ensures that all duties are aligned, the research deliverables are accurate and useful, and the user experience design is continuously improved based on real user needs.
column11:
For Analyzing Research Findings, the UX Research duty is both responsible for doing the work and accountable for its success, ensuring that data is carefully examined and insights are accurately identified. Product Ownership, Product Management, UX Strategy, UX Design, Business Analysis, and UX Writing duties are consulted, providing advice and feedback to help interpret findings and make sure the analysis aligns with business and user needs. All duties, including those in Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching, are informed about the results after the analysis is complete.

These duties work together by having UX Research lead the analysis, while consulted duties contribute their expertise to ensure findings are relevant and actionable. Once the analysis is done, all other duties are kept informed so they can use the insights in their own work. This collaboration ensures that research findings are accurate, support decision-making, and drive improvements in user experience design, with each duty playing a clear part in the process according to the RACI framework.

column5: Autotest Creation involves designing and developing automated test cases based on QA test plans and use case scenarios, requiring strong skills in attention to detail, logical thinking, and problem-solving to ensure tests accurately reflect requirements and catch defects. The duty demands collaboration and verbal and written communication to align with QA analysis and other stakeholders, as well as perseverance and commitment to refine tests through iterations. Effective time management and organization are essential to meet project milestones like quality assurance and launch. Practices such as decision-making, ownership, empowerment, and psychological safety foster a proactive and responsible approach, enhancing the quality and reliability of the autotests, which directly impacts the success of the QA analysis by providing thorough, repeatable, and efficient validation of software functionality.

column7: Autotest Creation is valuable for teams because it helps achieve deliverables like QA Test Cases, which are important for making sure everything works correctly during milestones such as Quality Assurance and Launch. This activity lets teams find and fix problems early, so the product is more reliable and ready for users when it’s time to launch.
column8: The QA Analysis duty works closely with Product Ownership and UX Design duties to deliver QA Test Cases during the Autotest Creation activity. QA Analysis uses input from Product Ownership, such as requirements and priorities, and collaborates with UX Design to understand user flows and interface details. By combining this information, QA Analysis designs and develops automated test cases that accurately reflect real user scenarios and product expectations. This collaboration ensures that the deliverables are thorough and aligned with both the intended product features and user experience, supporting the achievement of Quality Assurance and Launch milestones.
column9:
To be great at Autotest Creation while working with other cross-functional duties, the QA Analysis duty needs strong attention to detail, logical thinking, and problem-solving skills to design thorough and reliable automated test cases. Effective collaboration through clear verbal and written communication is essential for gathering input from Product Ownership and UX Design duties, ensuring that test cases accurately reflect requirements and user flows. Critical thinking, decision-making, and organization help prioritize and structure test scripts, while practices like ownership, self-organization, and empowerment foster commitment and perseverance for continuous improvement.

These skills and practices directly impact the success of delivering QA Test Cases within given milestones by ensuring that automated tests are comprehensive, maintainable, and aligned with both product expectations and user experience. By working cross-functionally and applying these abilities, the QA Analysis duty helps achieve Quality Assurance and Launch milestones on time, supporting a smooth project launch and high product quality.
column10:
Here's how the QA Analysis duty carries out the activity Autotest Creation with others  
Step 1  
QA Analysis gathers requirements and priorities from Product Ownership and reviews user flows and interface details with UX Design to fully understand the product features and user experience.  

Step 2  
QA Analysis uses this information to design and develop automated test cases, ensuring that each test accurately reflects real user scenarios and product expectations.  

Step 3  
QA Analysis organizes and documents the test scripts, communicates progress and findings clearly, and iteratively refines the tests based on feedback from Product Ownership and UX Design, ensuring the deliverables support Quality Assurance and Launch milestones.
column11:
In Autotest Creation, the QA Analysis duty is both responsible for performing the activity and accountable for its success, ensuring that automated test cases are designed, developed, and maintained to meet quality standards. The QA Analysis duty collaborates with Product Ownership and UX (User Experience) Design duties to gather requirements and understand user flows, while also consulting Business Analysis, Product Management, UX Research, and UX Writing duties for advice and feedback to refine the test cases. After the activity is completed, all duties—including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching—are informed about the work.

These duties work together by sharing information, providing feedback, and ensuring alignment with product requirements and user experience. The responsible and accountable QA Analysis duty leads the process, while consulted duties offer expertise to improve the quality and coverage of the tests. Informed duties stay updated on progress and outcomes, supporting transparency and coordination. This RACI structure ensures that Autotest Creation is thorough, reliable, and supports successful Quality Assurance and Launch milestones.

column5: Blockchain coding involves writing and testing code to create secure, transparent, and decentralized applications using blockchain programming languages and smart contracts. It requires skills such as attention to detail, algorithm design, command line scripting, object-oriented programming, and API integration, combined with critical thinking, problem-solving, and logical thinking to ensure the code functions correctly and efficiently. Effective collaboration, communication, and time management practices support decision-making, ownership, and empowerment, fostering psychological safety and service leadership within the development process. These skills and practices directly impact the quality and reliability of the deliverables, such as code and smart contracts, ultimately determining the success of the blockchain development activity.

column7: Blockchain Coding is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like Code and Smart Contracts, which are important for building and running blockchain applications during milestones such as Development and Development. This means that by doing Blockchain Coding, the duty can create the actual programs and smart rules that make blockchain systems work, and these are finished during the main building stages of a project.
column8: The development duty works closely with business analysis, product ownership, QA analysis, solutions architecture, and UX design duties to deliver code and smart contracts during the development milestones. The development duty relies on business analysis to clarify requirements and on product ownership to prioritize features, ensuring the code aligns with project goals. Solutions architecture provides technical guidance and structure, while UX design ensures the user experience is considered in the coding process. QA analysis collaborates by testing and reviewing the code for quality and security. column5: Choosing research methods involves selecting the most appropriate techniques like click testing and A/B prototype testing to gather user insights effectively. This duty requires skills such as critical thinking, decision-making, organization, and communication to create a clear research plan that guides the discovery process. Practices like ownership, psychological safety, empowerment, and agility ensure that decisions are made confidently and adaptively, fostering an environment where continuous growth and service leadership thrive. These skills and practices directly impact the success of the activity by enabling thorough, well-structured research that uncovers meaningful user experience insights, ultimately leading to better design decisions and improved product outcomes.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. https://www.sagepub.com/sites/default/files/upm-binaries/18594_040_Chapter3.pdf (SAGE Publications: "Choosing a Research Method")  
2. https://www.coursera.org/learn/research-methods (Coursera: "Understanding Research Methods" course)  
3. https://libguides.usc.edu/writingguide/researchdesigns (USC Libraries: "Organizing Your Social Sciences Research Paper: Types of Research Designs")
column7: Choosing Research Methods is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like a Research Plan, which guides how a team will learn about users and their needs during milestones such as Continuous Discovery. This activity is important because it helps the team pick the best ways to find answers to their questions, so they can make better decisions and build things that people actually want and need.
column8: The UX Research duty collaborates closely with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties to deliver a comprehensive Research Plan during the Choosing Research Methods activity. UX Research gathers input from Business Analysis to understand business needs, works with Product Management and Product Ownership to align research with product goals, and coordinates with UX Design and UX Strategy to ensure the chosen methods will inform design decisions and strategic direction. By sharing insights, communicating findings, and adapting research approaches based on feedback, these duties collectively support continuous discovery and ensure the Research Plan is thorough, actionable, and user-centered.
column9:
To be great at the UX Research duty when choosing research methods, it is important to have strong critical thinking, decision-making, and organizational skills. The duty must be able to analyze project needs, select the right techniques like Click Testing or A/B Prototype Testing, and clearly communicate the research plan to other cross-functional duties. Practices such as ownership, psychological safety, empowerment, and agility help the duty adapt to new information, encourage open feedback, and support continuous growth. Effective collaboration with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy ensures that the chosen methods align with business goals and design needs.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering the Research Plan within continuous discovery milestones. Clear communication and logical thinking help the duty gather and share insights efficiently, while strong organization and time management keep the research process on track. By adapting quickly to feedback and changes, the duty ensures that the research plan remains relevant and actionable, leading to well-informed design decisions and a better user experience. This approach supports the delivery of thorough, user-centered research plans that meet project goals and deadlines.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Research duty carries out the activity Choosing Research Methods with others  
Step 1  
The UX Research duty gathers input from Business Analysis, Product Management, and Product Ownership duties to understand business needs and product goals. This helps identify what user insights are needed and ensures the research will support the overall direction of the project.

Step 2  
The UX Research duty collaborates with UX Design and UX Strategy duties to select the most effective research methods, such as Click Testing or A/B Prototype Testing. Together, they discuss which techniques will best inform design decisions and align with strategic objectives, using critical thinking and communication skills to create a clear and actionable Research Plan.

Step 3  
The UX Research duty shares the Research Plan with all involved duties, communicates the chosen methods, and adapts the approach based on feedback. By practicing ownership, psychological safety, and agility, the duty ensures the research remains user-centered and responsive to new findings, supporting continuous discovery and well-informed design decisions.
column11:
The UX Research duty is both responsible and accountable for choosing research methods, meaning this duty does the work of selecting the best techniques and ensures the success of the activity. Product Ownership, Product Management, UX Strategy, UX Design, Business Analysis, and UX Writing duties are consulted, providing advice and feedback to help shape the research approach. All other duties, including those in development, design, security, and management, are informed about the chosen methods and outcomes after decisions are made.

These duties work together by having UX Research lead the process, gathering input from consulted duties to ensure the research plan aligns with business needs, product goals, and design strategy. Consulted duties share their expertise and feedback, which helps UX Research make well-informed decisions. Once the research methods are chosen, all relevant duties are kept informed, supporting transparency and alignment. This collaboration ensures the research plan is thorough, actionable, and user-centered, leading to better design decisions and continuous discovery.

column5: Clickable Prototype Creation involves designing interactive models of a product that simulate user experience through low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes and wireframes. This activity requires skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, problem-solving, logical thinking, and effective communication, both verbal and written, to clearly convey design ideas. Practices like decision-making, ownership, empowerment, and fostering psychological safety are essential to encourage creativity and confidence in making design choices. Collaboration and perseverance help in refining prototypes based on feedback, while organization and time management ensure milestones in experience design are met efficiently. These skills and practices directly impact the success of the UX design by creating prototypes that are intuitive, user-friendly, and aligned with user needs, ultimately enhancing the overall user experience.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. https://www.invisionapp.com/inside-design/how-to-create-clickable-prototype/  
2. https://www.adobe.com/creativecloud/design/discover/clickable-prototype.html  
3. https://uxdesign.cc/how-to-create-a-clickable-prototype-in-figma-6e2e7b2b1b6c
column7:
Clickable Prototype Creation is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like Low-Fidelity Prototypes, High-Fidelity Prototypes, and Wireframes, which help a team design and improve user experiences during milestones such as Experience Design.

In simple terms, Clickable Prototype Creation is a duty where people make simple or detailed models of apps or websites that you can actually click through, so everyone can see how things will work before they are built for real. This helps the team figure out what works best and make changes early, all during the important step called Experience Design.
column8: The UX Design duty works closely with Business Analysis, Product Ownership, and UX Research duties to deliver the prototypes and wireframes needed for the Clickable Prototype Creation activity. UX Design uses insights from UX Research to understand user needs and behaviors, while Business Analysis provides requirements and constraints that shape the prototype’s features and flow. Product Ownership ensures that the prototypes align with the overall product vision and priorities. By collaborating and sharing feedback, these duties help UX Design create low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes, as well as wireframes, that accurately represent the intended user experience and support the achievement of the Experience Design milestones.
column9:
To be great at Clickable Prototype Creation, the UX Design duty needs strong attention to detail to ensure every part of the prototype is accurate and realistic, as well as critical thinking and decision-making to solve design challenges and make choices that best serve the user. Collaboration and communication are essential for working with Business Analysis, Product Ownership, and UX Research duties, allowing the UX Design duty to gather requirements, understand user needs, and align with the product vision. Practices like ownership and self-organization help maintain focus and accountability, while psychological safety and empowerment encourage creativity and the willingness to try new ideas.

These skills and practices directly impact the success of delivering low-fidelity prototypes, high-fidelity prototypes, and wireframes within the Experience Design milestones. Effective communication and collaboration ensure that feedback is quickly incorporated, reducing delays and misunderstandings. Attention to detail and strong organization help the UX Design duty stay on track and meet deadlines, while critical thinking and problem-solving allow for quick adjustments when challenges arise. As a result, the deliverables are more likely to meet user needs and product goals, supporting the timely achievement of project milestones.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Design duty carries out the activity Clickable Prototype Creation with others  
Step 1  
UX Design gathers user insights from UX Research and requirements from Business Analysis to understand what users need and what features or constraints must be included in the prototype.

Step 2  
UX Design creates wireframes and low-fidelity prototypes, sharing them with Product Ownership and other duties for feedback to ensure the designs align with the product vision and priorities.

Step 3  
UX Design refines the prototypes into high-fidelity, interactive models, incorporating feedback from all cross-functional duties, and prepares them for user testing and further iteration.
column11:
For Clickable Prototype Creation, the UX (User Experience) Design duty is both responsible for performing the activity and accountable for its success, ensuring that interactive models accurately reflect user needs and product requirements. The consulted duties—UX Research, UX Writing, UX Strategy, Product Ownership, Product Management, Scrum Master, Development, Graphic Design, Solutions Architecture, and Information Security Analysis—provide valuable advice and feedback throughout the process, helping to refine the prototypes and address any design or technical challenges. All duties, including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching, are informed about the work after it is completed.

These duties work together by sharing insights, requirements, and feedback, which allows UX Design to create low-fidelity and high-fidelity prototypes and wireframes that support the Experience Design milestones. The responsible and accountable duty leads the activity, while consulted duties contribute expertise and informed duties stay updated, ensuring that the final prototypes are well-rounded, user-focused, and aligned with the overall product vision.

column5: Coaching Team Mates involves guiding individuals through Agile Coaching by applying skills such as conflict resolution, advocacy, building influence, and critical thinking to foster collaboration and continuous growth. The duty requires attention to detail, effective communication, and decision-making to create psychological safety and empowerment, encouraging ownership and service leadership. Practices like agility, perseverance, and expectation setting support the development of a Personal Growth Roadmap, which serves as a key deliverable. These skills and practices ensure that individuals are motivated, aligned with business goals, and capable of adapting to change, ultimately driving the success of the activity through improved teamwork, problem-solving, and commitment.

column7: Coaching Team Mates is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps achieve deliverables like a Personal Growth Roadmap, which supports everyone’s learning and improvement during milestones such as All Product Milestones and Intake. This activity makes sure that everyone knows how they can get better at their work and helps the group reach important goals together.
column8:
The Agile Coaching duty collaborates closely with other duties such as Account Management, Business Analysis, Development, and UX Design to deliver the Personal Growth Roadmap as part of the Coaching Team Mates activity. By fostering psychological safety, encouraging self-organization, and promoting effective communication, Agile Coaching helps individuals in these duties resolve conflicts, share insights, and align on goals. This collaboration ensures that each duty contributes its expertise and feedback, which is essential for creating a comprehensive and actionable Personal Growth Roadmap.

Throughout all product milestones and intake activities, Agile Coaching works with duties like Product Management, Project Management, QA Analysis, and Scrum Master to set clear expectations and track progress. By advocating for continuous improvement and empowering individuals to take ownership, Agile Coaching ensures that the deliverables not only support personal development but also drive collective success. This integrated approach helps all duties stay organized, persevere through challenges, and achieve the desired outcomes for each milestone.
column9:
To be great at the Coaching Team Mates activity, the Agile Coaching duty needs strong skills in conflict resolution, advocacy, collaboration, critical thinking, and both verbal and written communication. Practicing attention to detail, organization, and perseverance is essential for setting clear expectations and building influence across cross-functional duties. Agile Coaching must foster psychological safety, encourage self-organization and ownership, and practice service leadership to empower individuals. By promoting continuous growth and decision-making, Agile Coaching helps individuals feel supported and motivated to improve, while also generating buy-in for Agile practices and ensuring that the Personal Growth Roadmap is comprehensive and actionable.

These skills and practices directly impact the success of delivering the Personal Growth Roadmap within given milestones. Effective communication and collaboration with other duties such as Account Management, Business Analysis, Development, and UX Design ensure that expertise and feedback are integrated, leading to better alignment and shared goals. By advocating for continuous improvement and empowering individuals to take initiative, Agile Coaching helps all duties stay organized, overcome challenges, and remain committed to achieving product milestones and intake goals. This integrated approach ensures that deliverables support both personal development and collective success, driving the timely and successful completion of all milestones.
column10:

This involves working closely with duties such as Account Management, Business Analysis, Development, and UX Design to gather feedback and expertise needed for each person’s Personal Growth Roadmap.

Step 2

Step 3

This organized and persistent approach helps all duties persevere through challenges, stay aligned, and achieve the desired outcomes for each milestone.

column5: Collecting Analytics involves carefully gathering and analyzing data related to user interactions and behaviors to understand how users experience a product or service. This duty requires skills such as attention to detail, data analysis, critical thinking, and organization to ensure accurate and meaningful insights. Practices like decision-making, ownership, and empowerment help maintain focus and responsibility throughout the process, while collaboration and communication skills support sharing findings effectively. Deliverables include an Analytics Audit and a UX Audit Report, which provide a clear picture of user experience strengths and areas for improvement. Mastery of these skills and practices directly impacts the success of the activity by enabling precise identification of user needs and challenges, leading to better-informed design decisions and enhanced overall user satisfaction.

column7: Collecting Analytics is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like an Analytics Audit and a UX Audit Report, which help a team understand how people use a product or service during milestones such as Acceptance Testing, Continuous Discovery, and Requirements. This activity lets teams see what’s working well and what needs to be improved, so they can make better decisions and create things that people enjoy using.
column8: The duty owner for UX Research collaborates closely with other duties such as Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy to deliver the Analytics Audit and UX Audit Report. During milestones like Acceptance Testing, Continuous Discovery, and Requirements, the UX Research duty gathers and interprets user data, while Business Analysis helps define what data is needed and why. Product Management and Product Ownership duties ensure that the analytics align with business goals and product priorities, and UX Design and Strategy duties use the findings to inform design improvements and strategic decisions. By working together, these duties ensure that the collected analytics are accurate, relevant, and actionable, leading to deliverables that support better user experiences and informed decision-making throughout the project.
column9:
To be great at collecting analytics, the UX Research duty needs strong attention to detail, data analysis, critical thinking, and organization to ensure that user data is gathered accurately and interpreted meaningfully. Effective practices like decision-making, ownership, and empowerment help the duty stay focused and responsible, while collaboration and communication skills are essential for working with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties. Perseverance and time management are also important to complete thorough audits and reports within deadlines, and logical thinking, problem-solving, and accessibility awareness ensure that insights are inclusive and actionable.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering the Analytics Audit and UX Audit Report within project milestones such as Acceptance Testing, Continuous Discovery, and Requirements. By collaborating cross-functionally and maintaining clear communication, the UX Research duty ensures that analytics are relevant to business goals and product priorities, and that findings are clearly presented for design and strategy improvements. This leads to more accurate, timely, and actionable deliverables, supporting better user experiences and informed decisions throughout the project.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Research duty carries out the activity Collecting Analytics with others  
Step 1  
The UX Research duty works with Business Analysis to define what user data needs to be collected and why, ensuring the analytics will answer important questions about user behavior and product goals.  

Step 2  
The UX Research duty collaborates with Product Management and Product Ownership to make sure the analytics align with business priorities, and then gathers and analyzes user interaction data during project milestones like Acceptance Testing and Continuous Discovery.  

Step 3  
The UX Research duty shares findings in the form of an Analytics Audit and UX Audit Report with UX Design and UX Strategy, who use these insights to suggest design improvements and guide strategic decisions, ensuring the analytics collected are relevant and actionable for better user experiences.
column11:
For Collecting Analytics, the UX (User Experience) Research duty is both responsible for performing the activity and accountable for its success, ensuring that data is gathered and analyzed accurately. Product Ownership, Product Management, UX Strategy, UX Design, Business Analysis, and UX Writing duties are consulted, providing advice and feedback to guide the process and ensure the analytics align with business and user needs. After the analytics are collected and analyzed, all duties—including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching—are informed about the results and findings.

These duties work together by having UX Research lead the collection and interpretation of user data, while consulted duties contribute their expertise to shape the analytics approach and ensure relevance. Once the work is complete, informing all duties ensures transparency and enables everyone to use the insights for better decision-making. This collaboration ensures that analytics are accurate, actionable, and support continuous improvement in user experience and product development.

column5: Contextual Inquiries involve carefully observing and interviewing users in their natural environment to understand their behaviors, needs, and challenges. This activity requires skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, problem-solving, and strong verbal and written communication to accurately capture and analyze user experiences. Practices like decision-making, ownership, psychological safety, and empowerment help create a supportive environment where honest feedback is encouraged and insights are deeply explored. Effective organization, time management, and perseverance ensure thorough data collection and analysis, leading to a comprehensive research report. These skills and practices directly impact the success of the activity by enabling clear understanding of user contexts, which informs better design decisions and improves overall user experience.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. Nielsen Norman Group – Contextual Inquiry: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/contextual-inquiry/  
2. Interaction Design Foundation – Contextual Inquiry: https://www.interaction-design.org/literature/topics/contextual-inquiry  
3. Usability.gov – Contextual Inquiry: https://www.usability.gov/how-to-and-tools/methods/contextual-inquiry.html
column7: Contextual Inquiries are valuable for cross-functional agile teams because they help achieve deliverables like a Research Report, which gives important insights about how people use a product or service during milestones such as Continuous Discovery. This activity lets teams watch and talk to real users in their own environment, so they can understand what users need, what problems they face, and what works well for them. By doing this, teams can make better decisions and create products that are more helpful and easier to use.
column8: The UX Research duty works closely with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties to deliver a comprehensive research report during the continuous discovery milestone. While conducting contextual inquiries, the UX Research duty gathers detailed observations and user feedback, then collaborates with Business Analysis to interpret user needs and align findings with business goals. Product Management and Product Ownership duties help prioritize which user challenges to focus on, ensuring the research addresses the most valuable opportunities. UX Design and UX Strategy duties use the synthesized insights from the research report to inform design decisions and long-term planning, creating a cycle where each duty’s input strengthens the overall understanding of user experience and supports ongoing improvement.
column9:
To be great at contextual inquiries while working with other cross-functional duties, the UX Research duty needs strong attention to detail, critical thinking, and excellent verbal and written communication to accurately observe and document user behaviors. Collaboration, logical thinking, and time management are essential for working closely with Business Analysis, Product Management, Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Strategy duties. Practices like decision-making, ownership, psychological safety, and empowerment help the UX Research duty confidently engage with users, interpret findings, and share insights in a supportive environment.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering a clear and actionable research report within the continuous discovery milestone. Effective collaboration and communication ensure that user insights are aligned with business goals and design strategies, while strong organization and perseverance help synthesize complex data into meaningful recommendations. This enables all involved duties to make informed decisions quickly, prioritize valuable opportunities, and continuously improve the user experience, ensuring deliverables are met on time and with high quality.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Research duty carries out the activity Contextual Inquiries with others  
Step 1  
The UX Research duty plans and conducts contextual inquiries by observing and interviewing users in their natural environment, using attention to detail and verbal communication to gather accurate data about user behaviors and challenges.

Step 2  
After collecting observations, the UX Research duty collaborates with Business Analysis to interpret user needs and align findings with business goals, while working with Product Management and Product Ownership duties to prioritize which user challenges to address.

Step 3  
The UX Research duty synthesizes insights into a clear research report and shares it with UX Design and UX Strategy duties, who use the findings to inform design decisions and long-term planning, ensuring continuous improvement of the user experience.
column11:
For Contextual Inquiries, the UX (User Experience) Research duty is both responsible for conducting the research and accountable for the success of the activity. Product Ownership, Product Management, UX Strategy, UX Design, Business Analysis, and UX Writing duties are consulted, providing advice and feedback to ensure the research is relevant and thorough. All duties, including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching, are informed about the outcomes.

These duties work together by having UX Research lead the observation and analysis of user behaviors, while consulted duties offer insights and help align the research with business and design goals. Once the research report is complete, all informed duties receive the findings, enabling them to use the insights for better decision-making and continuous improvement. This collaboration ensures that user needs are understood and addressed, resulting in more effective and user-centered solutions.

column5: Copywriting involves creating clear, engaging, and persuasive text such as copywriting documents and microcopy that guide and inform users effectively. It requires skills like attention to detail, critical thinking, decision-making, organization, and strong written and verbal communication to craft messages that resonate with the audience. Practices such as ownership, service leadership, empowerment, and fostering psychological safety help ensure the copy is user-focused and aligned with project milestones like requirements and experience design. These skills and practices directly impact the success of UX writing by making content intuitive, accessible, and supportive of a positive user experience.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. Copyblogger (https://copyblogger.com)  
2. HubSpot’s Guide to Copywriting (https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/copywriting-tips)  
3. American Writers & Artists Institute (AWAI) (https://www.awai.com/copywriting/)
column7: Copywriting is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like the Copywriting Document and Microcopy, which are important for making sure the words and messages in a product are clear and helpful during milestones such as Requirements and Experience Design. This duty, owned by UX (User Experience) Writing, makes sure that everything people read in an app or website is easy to understand and guides users in the right way, helping the team create a better experience for everyone.
column8: The UX Writing duty collaborates closely with Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Research duties to deliver copywriting documents and microcopy that support project milestones like requirements and experience design. UX Writing relies on insights from UX Research to understand user needs and behaviors, and works with UX Design to ensure that the written content fits seamlessly within the visual and interactive elements of the product. Product Ownership provides direction on project goals and requirements, helping UX Writing align messaging with business objectives. By working together, these duties ensure that the copy is clear, persuasive, and user-focused, making digital products easier and more enjoyable to use.
column9:
To be great at UX Writing while working with other cross-functional duties, it is important to have strong attention to detail, critical thinking, and excellent written and verbal communication skills. Practices like ownership, empowerment, and service leadership help the UX Writing duty take responsibility for crafting clear, user-focused copy that fits seamlessly with the visual and interactive elements created by UX Design and is informed by insights from UX Research. Collaboration and logical thinking are essential for aligning the copy with project requirements and business objectives provided by Product Ownership, ensuring that every message is purposeful and resonates with users.

These skills and practices directly impact the ability to deliver copywriting documents and microcopy on time and at a high quality. By staying organized, making thoughtful decisions, and communicating effectively with other duties, UX Writing can meet project milestones such as requirements and experience design. This leads to content that is intuitive and engaging, helping users interact smoothly with digital products and contributing to the overall success of the project within the given deadlines.
column10:
Here's how the UX (User Experience) Writing duty carries out the activity Copywriting with others  
Step 1  
The UX Writing duty gathers insights from the UX Research duty to understand user needs, behaviors, and pain points, and reviews requirements provided by the Product Ownership duty to ensure alignment with project goals.  

Step 2  
The UX Writing duty collaborates with the UX Design duty to create copy that fits seamlessly within the visual and interactive elements of the product, ensuring the language is clear, engaging, and user-focused.  

Step 3  
The UX Writing duty refines and finalizes the copywriting document and microcopy, using feedback from Product Ownership, UX Design, and UX Research duties to make sure the content is persuasive, accessible, and supports the overall user experience.
column11:
For the activity of copywriting, the UX Writing duty is both responsible for creating the copy and accountable for its success. Duties such as UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, Business Analysis, Product Ownership, and Product Management are consulted to provide advice and feedback, ensuring the copy aligns with user needs, business goals, and project requirements. All duties, including those in development, design, analysis, management, and operations, are informed about the copywriting work once it is completed.

These duties work together by having UX Writing craft clear and persuasive content, while consulting duties offer insights and direction to make sure the copy fits seamlessly within the product and meets user and business objectives. Once the copy is finalized, all relevant duties are kept informed, ensuring transparency and alignment across the project. This collaboration ensures that the final copy is user-focused, effective, and supports the overall success of the digital product.

column5: Creating Acceptance Criteria involves defining clear, detailed conditions that a product or feature must meet to be considered complete and successful. This duty requires skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, logical thinking, and effective communication to ensure that all requirements are accurately captured and understood. Practices like decision-making, ownership, empowerment, and fostering psychological safety are essential to encourage collaboration and buy-in from stakeholders. Deliverables include acceptance criteria, task flows, use case scenarios, task-level scope, and use case diagrams, which guide development and testing. Mastery of these skills and practices ensures that acceptance criteria are precise and actionable, directly impacting the success of the project by reducing misunderstandings and aligning expectations.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. https://www.mountaingoatsoftware.com/agile/user-stories  
2. https://www.atlassian.com/agile/project-management/requirements/acceptance-criteria  
3. https://www.scrum.org/resources/blog/how-write-acceptance-criteria
column7: Creating Acceptance Criteria is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps achieve deliverables like Acceptance Criteria, Task Flows, Use Case Scenarios, Task-Level Scope, and Use Case Diagrams, which are important for reaching the Requirements milestone. This activity makes sure everyone understands what needs to be done, how tasks should work, and what success looks like, so the team can build the right thing and know when it’s finished.
column8: The duty owner for Business Analysis works closely with other duties such as Development, UX Writing, UX Strategy, UX Research, UX Design, Solutions Architecture, Scrum Master, QA Analysis, Project Management, Product Ownership, Product Operations, Product Management, Information Security Analysis, and Graphic Design to create clear and effective acceptance criteria. Business Analysis gathers input and feedback from these duties to ensure that acceptance criteria, task flows, use case scenarios, and task-level scope are accurate, realistic, and aligned with project requirements. By collaborating and communicating with these duties, Business Analysis refines deliverables so that everyone understands what needs to be built and tested, which helps prevent misunderstandings and ensures the product meets business goals at each milestone.
column9:
To be great at creating acceptance criteria while working with other cross-functional duties, Business Analysis needs strong attention to detail, critical and logical thinking, and effective verbal and written communication. These skills help ensure that acceptance criteria are clear, specific, and understandable for everyone involved. Practices such as decision-making, ownership, empowerment, and collaboration are essential for gathering input from duties like Development, UX, QA Analysis, and Product Management, and for refining the criteria so they align with business goals. Business Analysis must also be organized, persistent, and skilled at building influence and generating buy-in to make sure all perspectives are considered and the deliverables are realistic and measurable.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering acceptance criteria, task flows, and use case scenarios within given milestones. When Business Analysis communicates clearly and collaborates effectively with other duties, misunderstandings and rework are minimized, and everyone knows exactly what needs to be built and tested. This alignment helps ensure that deliverables are completed on time, meet requirements, and support the overall success of the project at each milestone.
column10:
Here's how the Business Analysis duty carries out the activity Creating Acceptance Criteria with others  
Step 1  
Business Analysis gathers input and requirements from cross-functional duties such as Development, UX Writing, UX Strategy, UX Research, UX Design, Solutions Architecture, QA Analysis, and Product Management to understand what the product or feature needs to achieve and what constraints or considerations exist.

Step 2  
Business Analysis uses critical thinking and attention to detail to draft clear, specific, and measurable acceptance criteria, task flows, and use case scenarios. During this step, Business Analysis collaborates with other duties to review and refine the criteria, ensuring that all perspectives are considered and that the criteria are realistic and aligned with business goals.

Step 3  
Business Analysis finalizes the acceptance criteria and related deliverables, communicates them to all involved duties, and ensures everyone understands what needs to be built and tested. This helps prevent misunderstandings and rework, supporting the successful completion of project milestones.
column11:
Business Analysis is both responsible for and accountable to creating acceptance criteria, meaning this duty does the work and ensures its success. Duties such as UX Design, UX Research, UX Writing, UX Strategy, Product Management, Scrum Master, Account Management, Development, and Solutions Architecture are consulted, providing advice and feedback to help shape clear, realistic, and effective acceptance criteria. Once the criteria are created, a wide range of duties—including Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching—are informed about the work.

All these duties work together by sharing expertise and feedback during the creation process, ensuring the acceptance criteria are thorough and aligned with business goals. Business Analysis gathers and integrates input from consulted duties, then communicates the final deliverables to all informed duties. This collaboration helps prevent misunderstandings, ensures everyone knows what needs to be built and tested, and supports the successful completion of project milestones.

column5: Creating AI prompts involves carefully crafting clear and precise instructions that guide the AI to generate useful and relevant responses. This duty requires skills such as attention to detail to ensure prompts are specific, critical thinking and logical thinking to anticipate how the AI will interpret the prompt, and problem-solving to refine prompts for better outcomes. Effective written communication is essential to express ideas clearly, while time management and organization help meet project milestones efficiently. Practices like decision-making, ownership, and empowerment encourage taking responsibility for prompt quality and continuous improvement. Collaboration and psychological safety foster an environment where feedback is shared openly, enhancing prompt effectiveness. These skills and practices directly impact the success of creating AI prompts by ensuring the prompts are well-constructed, purposeful, and aligned with the desired deliverables.
column6:
Here are some resources to learn about how to perform the activity:  
1. OpenAI Cookbook – Prompt Engineering: https://github.com/openai/openai-cookbook/blob/main/techniques_to_improve_reliability.md  
2. DeepLearning.AI – ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers (free course): https://www.deeplearning.ai/short-courses/chatgpt-prompt-engineering-for-developers/  
3. Google Developers – Introduction to Prompt Design: https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/prompt-design
column7: Creating AI Prompts is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps achieve deliverables like AI Prompts, which are essential for building, testing, and improving AI features during all Product Milestones. This activity lets teams clearly define what they want the AI to do, making it easier to create smart tools or services that work well and solve real problems. By making good AI Prompts, teams can reach important milestones like planning, building, testing, and launching new products or updates.
column8:
The duty owner collaborates closely with other duties by sharing insights, feedback, and expertise to ensure that AI prompts are crafted with clarity and precision. Each duty contributes unique skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, and effective communication, which are essential for refining prompts and anticipating how the AI will respond. Through ongoing interaction and open exchange of ideas, the duties work together to review, test, and improve the prompts, ensuring they meet the required standards and align with project goals.

By coordinating their efforts, the duties collectively take responsibility for the quality and relevance of the AI prompts, supporting each other in meeting deadlines and achieving all product milestones. This collaborative approach allows for continuous improvement, encourages creativity, and ensures that the deliverables are effective and valuable for the intended purpose.
column9:
To be great at creating AI prompts while working with other cross-functional duties, it is important to have strong attention to detail, critical thinking, and logical problem-solving skills. Duties must practice clear verbal and written communication to share ideas and feedback, as well as perseverance and initiative to refine prompts through trial and error. Effective organization and time management help structure prompts logically and ensure deadlines are met. Practicing ownership, decision-making, service leadership, psychological safety, and empowerment encourages each duty to take responsibility for prompt quality and fosters an environment where creativity and experimentation are supported.

These skills and practices directly impact the success of delivering high-quality AI prompts within given milestones. By collaborating and sharing expertise, duties can continuously improve prompt clarity and relevance, leading to more effective AI-generated outputs. This collective approach ensures that deliverables meet required standards, align with project goals, and are completed on time, supporting the achievement of all product milestones.
column10:
Here's how the All duties duty carries out the activity Creating AI Prompts with others  
Step 1  
All duties begin by discussing the goals and requirements for the AI prompt, sharing insights and feedback to ensure everyone understands what is needed. Each duty uses attention to detail and critical thinking to suggest clear and specific instructions, while also considering how the AI might interpret the prompt.

Step 2  
All duties review and test the drafted prompts together, using collaboration and communication skills to identify areas for improvement. They openly exchange ideas, provide constructive feedback, and refine the prompts through trial and error, making sure the instructions are unambiguous and effective.

Step 3  
All duties take ownership of the final prompt quality by coordinating their efforts to meet deadlines and achieve project milestones. They support each other by encouraging creativity, maintaining psychological safety, and ensuring the deliverables are relevant and valuable for the intended purpose.
column11:
For the activity of creating AI prompts, all duties are responsible for performing the work, overseeing its success, and providing advice and feedback throughout the process. After the prompts are created, all duties, along with those in Business Analysis, Development, Graphic Design, Information Security Analysis, Product Management, Product Operations, Product Ownership, Project Management, QA Analysis, Scrum Master, Solutions Architecture, UX Design, UX Research, UX Strategy, UX Writing, Account Management, and Agile Coaching, are informed about the results.

These duties work together by sharing insights, giving feedback, and using their unique skills such as attention to detail, critical thinking, and communication to refine and improve the AI prompts. By coordinating their efforts, they ensure the prompts are clear, relevant, and effective, which helps achieve all product milestones. This collaborative approach encourages creativity, supports continuous improvement, and ensures the deliverables meet the intended goals.

column5: Creating annotations involves clearly documenting design decisions and UI specifications to guide development, ensuring that every element is explained with precision. This duty requires skills in writing detailed UI specifications and understanding how design choices impact functionality and user interaction. Practices such as decision-making, ownership, and empowerment are essential, as they help maintain clarity and confidence in the annotations, while psychological safety encourages open communication and feedback. These skills and practices directly affect the success of the activity by producing thorough, understandable design annotations that improve collaboration and result in a smoother, more intuitive user experience.
column6:

1. https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/annotation-python-matplotlib (DataCamp: How to Annotate Plots in Python)

column7: Creating Annotations is valuable for cross-functional agile teams because it helps to achieve deliverables like UI Development Specifications and Design Annotation, which are important for making sure everyone understands how the product should look and work during milestones such as Requirements. This activity makes it easier for everyone to be on the same page about what needs to be built and how it should function.
column8: The UX Design duty collaborates closely with Business Analysis, Product Ownership, and UX Research duties to deliver clear UI Development Specifications and Design Annotations within the requirements milestone. While UX Design focuses on writing precise annotations and ensuring the design intent is fully understood, Business Analysis provides detailed business requirements, Product Ownership clarifies priorities and goals, and UX Research offers user insights that inform annotation details. By working together, these duties ensure that all annotations are accurate, relevant, and aligned with both user needs and business objectives, resulting in deliverables that guide developers to build intuitive and effective user interfaces.
column9:
To be great at creating annotations, the UX Design duty needs strong skills in writing clear and detailed UI specifications, as well as a deep understanding of the design intent. Effective practices include decision-making and ownership to ensure annotations are accurate and consistent, while psychological safety and empowerment encourage open communication and confidence in documenting requirements. Self-organization and service leadership help the duty stay thorough and timely, making sure all necessary details are included for developers to follow.

These skills and practices directly affect the success of delivering UI Development Specifications and Design Annotations within the requirements milestone. When the UX Design duty collaborates well with Business Analysis, Product Ownership, and UX Research, the annotations become more accurate and aligned with both user needs and business goals.
